skill.name: longbridge-watchlist
skill.description:
Watchlist group management (list/create/rename/delete/add/remove symbols), price alerts (list/add/delete), and community stock lists (sharelist: list/detail/create/delete/manage) via Longbridge. Mutating operations require explicit user confirmation (dry-run protocol).
Triggers: "自选股", "添加自选", "删除自选", "创建分组", "价格提醒", "提醒", "股票清单", "自選股", "新增至自選", "建立分組", "價格提醒", "watchlist", "add to watchlist", "create group", "rename group", "price alert", "sharelist", "community list", "刪除自選", "股票清單", "重命名分組", "重新命名"
skill.summary: Manage Longbridge watchlist groups, price alerts, and community stock lists with multilingual triggers and mandatory preview-then-confirm for mutations.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: All 96 static findings are false positives. The skill is prompt-only with no executable code, no scripts, and no network calls. The 83 'Ruby/shell backtick' detections are markdown code-span formatting in documentation (e.g. `longbridge watchlist create`). The 'weak crypto' and 'system recon' flags are language-based false positives triggered by words like 'OAuth', 'auth', and 'login' in prose. The two 'hardcoded URL' detections are legitimate documentation links to the longbridge-terminal GitHub repo. The skill instructs the LLM to call the Longbridge CLI at runtime and follow a mandatory two-step preview-then-confirm protocol for all mutating operations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in documentation reference
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged URLs to https://github.com/longportapp/longbridge-terminal in references/alert.md:57 and references/watchlist.md:62. These are legitimate documentation links to the Longbridge CLI repository, not malicious network endpoints. No network request is made by the skill itself.

content.user_title: Manage Longbridge Watchlists and Price Alerts
content.value_statement: Track stocks and get price alerts across multiple markets with natural-language commands. The skill handles watchlist groups, price alerts, and community stock lists with built-in safety for every mutation.
actual_capabilities: List, create, rename, and delete watchlist groups on Longbridge
Add or remove stock symbols from watchlist groups by ticker
List, add, enable, disable, and delete price alerts
Browse popular and subscribed community stock lists (sharelist)
View constituents and details of any community stock list
Create, manage, and delete your own community stock lists
limitations: Requires longbridge-terminal CLI installed and user logged in with valid OAuth scope
Mutating operations (create/delete/rename) cannot be combined with read operations in one turn
Cannot place trades — price alerts only send push notifications, no order execution
Ticker format must follow CODE.MARKET convention (e.g. TSLA.US, 700.HK)

use_cases.title: Organize portfolio watchlists
use_cases.description: Create themed groups like 'Tech Stocks' or 'Dividend Picks' and move symbols between them.
use_cases.target_user: Retail investor managing a multi-thesis portfolio

use_cases.title: Set price-triggered alerts
use_cases.description: Add rise or fall price alerts on key tickers and get notified when targets are hit.
use_cases.target_user: Active trader monitoring entry and exit levels

use_cases.title: Curate community stock lists
use_cases.description: Browse popular community lists or create your own curated list to share with other Longbridge users.
use_cases.target_user: Investment community member or content creator

prompt_templates.title: View all watchlist groups
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all my watchlist groups.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting a quick overview of tracked stocks

prompt_templates.title: Add symbols to a group
prompt_templates.prompt: Add NVDA.US, AAPL.US, and TSLA.US to my 'Tech Stocks' watchlist group.
prompt_templates.scenario: Populating a new or existing group with multiple symbols

prompt_templates.title: Set a price alert
prompt_templates.prompt: Set a price alert for 700.HK at 300 HKD when the price rises above that level.

prompt_templates.title: Browse community stock lists
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the top 10 popular community stock lists on Longbridge.
prompt_templates.scenario: Discovering curated lists from other investors

output_examples.input: Show me all my watchlist groups.
output: 1. Tech Stocks (12 symbols) — NVDA.US, AAPL.US, TSLA.US, ...
2. Dividend Picks (8 symbols) — KO.US, JNJ.US, PG.US, ...

output_examples.input: Set a price alert for TSLA.US at 250 USD, triggered when price falls below.
output_examples.output: About to add a price alert on TSLA.US at 250 USD, triggered when price falls below. Confirm?

output_examples.input: Show me the top 5 popular community stock lists.
output: 1. AI & Semiconductors — owner: trader_xyz — 1,240 subscribers — 32 symbols
2. Hong Kong Blue Chips — owner: hk_investor — 980 subscribers — 28 symbols
3. US High Dividend — owner: dividend_fan — 875 subscribers — 45 symbols

best_practices: Always run `longbridge <subcommand> --help` before invoking a command to verify current flag names and argument order
Use the mandatory two-step preview-then-confirm protocol for every mutating operation — never skip the confirmation turn
When given a group name instead of an id, look it up first with a read call before attempting mutations
anti_patterns: Combining a preview and its execution in a single turn — mutations must wait for explicit user confirmation
Piping 'yes' or passing flags that bypass the CLI's built-in delete confirmation prompt
Defaulting to 'rise' or 'fall' direction for price alerts when the user did not specify one — always ask

faq.question: Does this skill place trades or move money?
faq.answer: No. The skill manages watchlists, price alerts, and community stock lists. It does not place orders or move funds. Price alerts only send push notifications.

faq.question: What OAuth scope is required?
faq.answer: Reading requires Quote permission. Mutating watchlist groups requires the Trade scope. Price alerts use the basic login scope.

faq.question: What happens if the longbridge CLI is not installed?
faq.answer: The skill tells the user to install longbridge-terminal from GitHub. An MCP fallback may apply, but only after user confirmation and never to bypass the preview-confirm protocol.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple mutations in one command?
faq.answer: No. Each mutating operation must run as a separate preview and confirmation turn. The skill enforces this to prevent accidental changes.

faq.question: What ticker format should I use?
faq.answer: Use the CODE.MARKET format, for example TSLA.US for Tesla on US markets or 700.HK for Tencent on Hong Kong exchanges.

faq.question: How do community stock lists (sharelist) work?
faq.answer: Sharelists are curated collections of symbols that Longbridge users can publish and subscribe to. You can browse popular lists, view details, or create your own.
